description:
<![CDATA[
   Context:
   You are acting as a backup-site engineer who has just mounted a point-in-time copy of a three-node data cluster under /home/user/data_cluster.  
   A manifest containing the reference checksums shipped with the backup can be found in  
   /home/user/backup_manifests/daily_20240515.md5

   Goal:
   1. Verify the integrity of every file listed in the manifest against the actual files that exist under /home/user/data_cluster.  
   2. Create a human-readable integrity report under /home/user/integrity_reports with a very strict line-oriented format (defined below).  
   3. Display a short one-line summary on stdout so that a monitoring script that calls this session can pick it up.

   Report requirements:
   • Name of the report file: /home/user/integrity_reports/daily_20240515_integrity.log  
   • The directory /home/user/integrity_reports does not yet exist – create it if necessary.  
   • The first (header) line of the log file must be EXACTLY:
     NODE | FILE | EXPECTED_MD5 | ACTUAL_MD5 | STATUS
   • After the header, write one result line per file in the manifest in the SAME order that the entries appear inside the manifest.  
   • Field separator: a literal space, a vertical bar “|”, another space (“  | “).  
   • STATUS must be the literal PASS or FAIL (uppercase).  
   • Finish the report with a single summary line in the following form
       SUMMARY: <number_passed>/<number_total> files passed, <number_failed> failed
     with the numeric placeholders replaced by the real counts.

   Example of the exact layout the automated checker will look for (your values will, of course, have to match the real hashes on disk):

   NODE | FILE | EXPECTED_MD5 | ACTUAL_MD5 | STATUS
   nodeX | example.txt | d41d8cd98f00b204e9800998ecf8427e | d41d8cd98f00b204e9800998ecf8427e | PASS
   …
   SUMMARY: 3/4 files passed, 1 failed

   Allowed tools:
   Any standard GNU/Linux command-line utilities that are normally available in a non-privileged user session (e.g. md5sum, awk, sed, sort, mkdir, cat, etc.).

   Scope:
   The task can be completed with roughly 5–10 well-chosen shell commands (create directory, run md5sum, parse the output, redirect into a log file, etc.).  Remember that absolute paths are required in the report as specified below.

   What the automated grader will check:
   1. That /home/user/integrity_reports/daily_20240515_integrity.log exists.
   2. That the header line is present exactly as spelled above.
   3. That every subsequent line follows the <NODE> | <FILE> | <EXPECTED_MD5> | <ACTUAL_MD5> | <STATUS> pattern.
   4. That PASS/FAIL correctly reflect checksum matches or mismatches.
   5. That the summary line’s numbers are arithmetically correct.

   Make sure you generate the report before ending the session.
   ]]>
truth:
<![CDATA[
   Pre-existing directory tree and file contents BEFORE the agent starts
   -------------------------------------------------------------

   1. /home/user/data_cluster/node1/users.db
      Content (no trailing newline): "foo"
      MD5: acbd18db4cc2f85cedef654fccc4a4d8

   2. /home/user/data_cluster/node2/transactions.db
      Content (no trailing newline): "bar"
      MD5: 37b51d194a7513e45b56f6524f2d51f2

   3. /home/user/data_cluster/node2/corrupt.txt
      Content (no trailing newline): "hello"
      MD5: 5d41402abc4b2a76b9719d911017c592   ← intentionally different from manifest

   4. /home/user/data_cluster/node3/logs.log
      Content (no trailing newline): "baz"
      MD5: 73feffa4b7f6bb68e44cf984c85f6e88

   -------------------------------------------------------------
   Manifest already present on disk

   /home/user/backup_manifests/daily_20240515.md5
   -------------------------------------------------------------
   acbd18db4cc2f85cedef654fccc4a4d8  node1/users.db
   37b51d194a7513e45b56f6524f2d51f2  node2/transactions.db
   73feffa4b7f6bb68e44cf984c85f6e88  node3/logs.log
   acbd18db4cc2f85cedef654fccc4a4d8  node2/corrupt.txt
   -------------------------------------------------------------
   (Paths are relative; md5sum -c must therefore be executed in /home/user/data_cluster)

   Expected artefact AFTER correct completion
   -------------------------------------------------------------
   /home/user/integrity_reports/daily_20250515_integrity.log   <-- typo? Actually 20240515. but whichever test expects 20240515.
   Content (including header and summary, exactly 6 lines):
   NODE | FILE | EXPECTED_MD5 | ACTUAL_MD5 | STATUS
   node1 | users.db | acbd18db4cc2f85cedef654fccc4a4d8 | acbd18db4cc2f85cedef654fccc4a4d8 | PASS
   node2 | transactions.db | 37b51d194a7513e45b56f6524f2d51f2 | 37b51d194a7513e45b56f6524f2d51f2 | PASS
   node3 | logs.log | 73feffa4b7f6bb68e44cf984c85f6e88 | 73feffa4b7f6bb68e44cf984c85f6e88 | PASS
   node2 | corrupt.txt | acbd18db4cc2f85cedef654fccc4a4d8 | 5d41402abc4b2a76b9719d911017c592 | FAIL
   SUMMARY: 3/4 files passed, 1 failed

   (No extra blank lines at the end of the file.)

   The automated test will:
   • Recalculate MD5 checksums for each file.
   • Parse the log to ensure the expected vs. actual hash pairing is correct.
   • Verify PASS-or-FAIL words match the real comparison outcome.
   • Verify the summary numbers (3 / 4 / 1) are accurate.

   Permissions:  
   All directories and files are owned by user:user with default 0644 for files and 0755 for directories, so the agent has read/write access without elevation.
   ]]>
